h1: Low-Income Senior Programs in Alabama (2026)
introText: Find assistance programs for low-income seniors in Alabama. See SNAP, LIHEAP, housing assistance, and other benefits available in 2026.

programs.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
programs.description: Monthly food assistance for eligible seniors

programs.name: LIHEAP
programs.description: Help with heating and cooling bills

programs.name: Section 8 Housing
programs.description: Rental assistance vouchers for affordable housing

programs.name: Lifeline Program
programs.description: Discounted phone and internet service
programs.eligibility: Income below 135% FPL or Medicaid enrollment

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance
programs.description: Free home energy efficiency improvements

housingInfo.waitlistInfo: Waitlists are common; apply early

propertyTaxRelief.description: Alabama offers property tax relief programs for qualifying seniors.

transportationPrograms.name: Alabama Senior Transportation
transportationPrograms.description: Subsidized transportation for medical appointments and errands

faqs.question: What assistance programs are available for low-income seniors in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Alabama offers SNAP food assistance, LIHEAP energy assistance, housing vouchers, Lifeline phone/internet discounts, and other programs for qualifying seniors.

faqs.question: How do I apply for SNAP in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Apply online through Alabama's SNAP portal, by phone, or in person at your local office. You'll need proof of income, identity, and residency.
